mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate below 0.25% eliminates the risk-free yield alternative to equities and reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The mean 20-day SPX return when FEDFUNDS < 0.25% is not statistically greater than the unconditional mean over any 2-year rolling window (t-test p > 0.05), or the correlation between FEDFUNDS and forward returns is positive.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 9.0% signals weak household income formation and depressed consumer demand, which drags on corporate revenue expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE > 9.0%, S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances over a 3-year walk-forward window, or the mean conditional return is not negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Near-zero policy rates combined with a CPI level above 210 reflect an aggressively accommodative real monetary stance that reflates asset values; the interaction between these series sustains equity risk appetite and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL does not add incremental explanatory power (F-test p > 0.05) beyond main effects in predicting 20-day SPX returns, or conditional returns are not positive in > 55% of joint signal instances.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production below its 2017 capacity benchmark indicates substantial spare manufacturing capacity; in this sub-capacity regime, demand increments accrue to corporate margins rather than input costs, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When INDPRO < 100, the mean 60-day SPX forward return is not positive over a 2-year rolling window (t-test p > 0.10), or the correlation between INDPRO and forward returns is negative.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts below 550 thousand units signal a severely depressed construction sector with significant pent-up demographic demand; this extreme trough generates a supply-constrained rebound in residential investment that drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When HOUST < 550, 60-day forward SPX returns are negative in the majority of triggered instances over a 3-year window, or the mean conditional return is not positive (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: An extremely low federal funds rate signals acute financial stress and deficient credit demand that overwhelms the portfolio rebalancing channel, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances over a 2-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production below 95 indicates a manufacturing sector in severe contraction; this sharp supply-side deterioration precedes negative earnings revisions and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When INDPRO < 95, 5-day SPX forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances over a 3-year window, or the mean conditional return is not negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment below 132000 thousand signals a labor market with substantial room for job growth before reaching full employment; in this regime, hiring acceleration drives wage expectations and consumption, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When PAYEMS < 132000, the mean 60-day SPX forward return is not positive over a 2-year rolling window (t-test p > 0.10), or positive returns occur in fewer than 55% of triggered months.
